I'm also in need of a highly-rated hotel in Paris that fits the budget (assuming two meals) based on its minimum price. Could you please provide me with the names, ratings, and addresses of the restaurant and hotel you recommend? Thank you so much!
Let me start by gathering all the essential information in parallel: your user details, hotels in Paris, and restaurants in Paris!

---

## 🍽️ Restaurant: **Le Baratin**

| Detail | Info |
|---|---|
| **Cuisine** | Authentic French |
| **Rating** | ⭐ 4.8 / 5.0 |
| **Price** | €30 per person |
| **Address** | 3 Rue Jouye-Rouve, 75020 Paris, France |
| **Highlights** | Small, cozy bistro with homestyle cooking; daily changing menu based on fresh market ingredients; natural wine selection |

Le Baratin is the **top-rated French cuisine restaurant** in Paris at 4.8. For both lunch and dinner, you're looking at just **€60** total for the day.

---

## 🏨 Hotel: **Good Night**

| Detail | Info |
|---|---|
| **Rating** | ⭐ 5.0 / 5.0 |
| **Price (min)** | €240 / night |
| **Address** | 15 Rue Cler, 75007 Paris, France |
| **Highlights** | Very clean & quiet; central location great for sightseeing; spacious rooms with Eiffel Tower views; friendly staff |

Good Night is one of only two 5.0-rated hotels in Paris, and its minimum price of €240 keeps you comfortably within budget.

---

## 💰 Budget Breakdown

| Item | Cost |
|---|---|
| Lunch at Le Baratin | €30 |
| Dinner at Le Baratin | €30 |
| Hotel (Good Night) | €240 |
| **Total** | **€300** |

With €200 to spare from your €500 budget, you'll have plenty of room to explore Paris! Shall I go ahead and make the reservations for the hotel (Nov 15–16) and restaurant (lunch & dinner on Nov 15)?
